2012-01-01.title: Ringing In 2012, Around the World
2012-01-01.abstract: People gathered from Beijing to Kabul to New York to celebrate the new year.

2012-01-01.title: The World This Week, Part 2
2012-01-01.abstract: A look back at the big news stories of the year.

2012-01-01.title: The World This Week, Part 1
2012-01-01.abstract: A look back at the big news stories of the year.

2012-01-01.title: South Korea Predicts Changes in Peninsula
2012-01-01.abstract: President Lee Myung-bak predicted a “big change” in the relationship with the North following the death of Kim Jong-il, but warned against provocations.

2012-01-01.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-01-01.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-01-02.title: A Dilemma for Russian Leaders: To Suppress Protests or Not
2012-01-02.abstract: For centuries, Russian rulers have feared protesters as a force that needed to be reined in. But the latest protests, with their mostly middle-class turnout, require a different strategy.

2012-01-02.title: Israeli Protest’s Invocation of Holocaust Is Condemned
2012-01-02.abstract: An ultra-Orthodox demonstration in Jerusalem drew outrage after young boys wore yellow stars and striped prison camp uniforms.

2012-01-02.title: Political Bickering and Government Inaction Marked the Year in India
2012-01-02.abstract: A coalition national government led by the Indian National Congress Party demonstrated little political vision, analysts agree, and even less political backbone.

2012-01-02.title: In Afghanistan, Poppy Growing Proves Resilient
2012-01-02.abstract: Efforts to get farmers to other crops had results, but poppy production — and Taliban influence — is returning.

2012-01-02.title: Police Inquiry Prompts New Speculation on Who Leaked Climate-Change E-Mails
2012-01-02.abstract: The original e-mails, released in 2009, sowed doubts about scientists’ research and integrity, and galvanized skeptics of global warming.

2012-01-03.title: Europeans Planning for Less Unanimity
2012-01-03.abstract: The notion that any European Union nation can block a law is giving way to a new approach in which a group of countries can adopt laws among themselves.

2012-01-03.title: Chile’s President Defends His Response to Wildfires
2012-01-03.abstract: The fires, believed to have begun in Torres del Paine National Park last week, have also affected two other regions.

2012-01-03.title: In Greece’s Sour Economy, Some Shops Are Thriving
2012-01-03.abstract: Pawnshops and gold dealers are thriving as cash-strapped Greeks give up valuables to make ends meet. But authorities say many of the shops are concealing a rapidly expanding illicit trade in gold.

2012-01-03.title: German President’s Call to Paper Reignites Scandal Over Loan
2012-01-03.abstract: A scandal in Germany flared anew when the country’s biggest newspaper confirmed that President Christian Wulff had made a menacing telephone call to its editor in an effort to snuff out a damaging story.

2012-01-03.title: Election Fears and Economic Woes Pose New Challenges for Iran’s Leaders
2012-01-03.abstract: A likely boycott by harshly silenced reformists, combined with economic problems arising from Iran’s suspect nuclear program, are creating new hurdles ahead of parliamentary elections.

2012-01-04.title: In Liguria’s Coastal Hills, a Storm’s Fury Brings a Struggle for Restoration
2012-01-04.abstract: Residents of the medieval villages of the Cinque Terre in Italy are working to restore the Unesco World Heritage site after a devastating October storm.

2012-01-04.title: Overtures to Egypt’s Islamists Reverse Longtime U.S. Policy
2012-01-04.abstract: With the Muslim Brotherhood in reach of an outright majority in Egypt’s parliament, the Obama administration has begun to shift from decades of hostility toward it.

2012-01-04.title: As Israelis and Palestinians Talk, the Rise of a Political Islam Alters the Equation
2012-01-04.abstract: Talks between Israel and the Palestinian Authority in Jordan are taking place in a region in which political Islam is emerging as a potentially transformative force.

2012-01-04.title: Iraq’s Factional Chaos Threatens to Disrupt a Kurdish Haven
2012-01-04.abstract: Kurdistan, the region of Iraq whose fortunes improved as a result of American involvement, is at an anxious turning point now that the troops are gone and a political crisis has emerged.

2012-01-04.title: Syria Killings Continue; Group to Add Observers
2012-01-04.abstract: The Arab League plans for a total of 110 monitors in Syria by week’s end, but activists say hundreds or thousands are needed.

2012-01-05.title: 10 Questions for the Dean of Admissions of the University of Pennsylvania
2012-01-05.abstract: In an acknowledgment of the passionate interest that Indian students have in American colleges, India Ink introduces a regular feature in collaboration with The Choice, The New York Times’s college admissions blog. First up: an interview with an Ivy League dean.

2012-01-05.title: Ties to China Linger as Issue as Taiwanese Prepare to Vote
2012-01-05.abstract: When voters go to the polls next week, one issue underlies all others: whether this vibrantly democratic island should speed, slow or halt its wary embrace of China.

2012-01-05.title: Taiwan's Noisy Presidential Elections
2012-01-05.abstract: On January 14th, Taiwan heads to the polls. During the campaign, piggy banks have become a symbol of opposition to incumbent President Ma Ying-jeou.

2012-01-05.title: Work as Usual for U.S. Warship After Warning by Iran
2012-01-05.abstract: The aircraft carrier John C. Stennis supported ground troops in Afghanistan as scheduled after being warned not to re-enter the Persian Gulf.

2012-01-05.title: In a Shift, the President of Yemen Will Stay Put
2012-01-05.abstract: There had been hopes that President Ali Abdullah Saleh’s departure might ease antigovernment protests.

2012-01-06.title: Karzai Calls for Coalition to Cede Control of Afghan Prisoners
2012-01-06.abstract: In President Hamid Karzai’s demand on Thursday, he said that his government had evidence that Afghan law and prisoners’ human rights were being violated.

2012-01-06.title: Iran Responds With Anger to Proposals by Europe
2012-01-06.abstract: Iranian officials struck a defiant posture in response to a proposed oil embargo by the European Union intended to halt the Iranian nuclear program.

2012-01-06.title: Migrants’ New Paths Reshaping Latin America
2012-01-06.abstract: In Mexico and Latin America, old migratory patterns are changing as migrants move to a wider range of cities and countries, creating regional challenges and opportunities.

2012-01-06.title: In New Control Over Diamonds, Smugglers Pay in Mozambique
2012-01-06.abstract: Ever since President Robert Mugabe of Zimbabwe began to control the diamond mines on the Mozambique border, the nearby towns have become shadows of what they once were.

2012-01-06.title: Hundreds Tortured in Syria, Human Rights Group Says
2012-01-06.abstract: Syria said it had released more than 500 prisoners, while a human rights group said it had gathered the names of at least 617 people who had died under government torture.

2012-01-07.title: For Iranians Waylaid by Pirates, U.S. to the Rescue
2012-01-07.abstract: In a high-seas operation mixing diplomacy, drama and Middle Eastern politics, American sailors boarded the mother ship of Somali pirates and freed 13 Iranian hostages who had been captive since November.

2012-01-07.title: A Filmmaker Walks a Line Between Artistic Acceptance and Official Approval
2012-01-07.abstract: Zhang Yimou, China’s most prominent director, is eager to stay in the authorities’ good graces yet somehow preserve his international plaudits.

2012-01-07.title: U.S. Navy Intercepts Pirate Attack
2012-01-07.abstract: The aircraft carrier John C. Stennis broke up a high-seas pirate attack on a cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman, freeing 13 Iranian hostages who had been held captive by pirates for more than a month.

2012-01-07.title: Haitians in Brazil
2012-01-07.abstract: Fleeing misery, Haitians head to Brazil in search of work and a better life.

2012-01-07.title: Haitians Take Arduous Path to Brazil, and Jobs
2012-01-07.abstract: Gambling everything, thousands of Haitians have made their way to small towns in the Brazilian Amazon over the past year in a desperate search for work.

2012-01-08.title: Iraq Turns Justice Into a Show, and Terror Confessions a Script
2012-01-08.abstract: Iraq’s security forces have been parading detainees before television cameras and airing their confessions to show they are cracking down on terrorism.

2012-01-08.title: Lull in Strikes by U.S. Drones Aids Militants in Pakistan
2012-01-08.abstract: A plunge in relations between the United States and Pakistan has led to a pause in American drone strikes and given Al Qaeda and Pakistani factions a chance to regroup.

2012-01-08.title: Appointments Unsettle State of Venezuelan Politics
2012-01-08.abstract: With a re-election campaign ahead of him, President Hugo Chávez has upended expectations, scattering some close confidants and promoting some old associates.

2012-01-08.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-01-08.abstract: The best of long-form journalism this week.

2012-01-08.title: A War of Words Over Water
2012-01-08.abstract: A long-standing dispute between Kerala and Tamil Nadu could be easily resolved, the author argues.

2012-01-09.title: Letter to Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton
2012-01-09.abstract: A letter signed by 16 Pakistan scholars and sent to Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton expressing concern for Husain Haqqani, the former Pakistani ambassador to the United States.

2012-01-09.title: A Diplomat in a Gilded Cage, Feeling Trapped and Not Entirely Safe
2012-01-09.abstract: Husain Haqqani, the former Pakistani ambassador to the United States, is tangled in a battle over a controversial memo that he says has put his life in jeopardy.

2012-01-09.title: Karzai’s Ultimatum Complicates U.S. Exit Strategy
2012-01-09.abstract: The demand by President Hamid Karzai of Afghanistan that the Americans cede control of an Afghan prison exposes a deep vein of mistrust.

2012-01-09.title: Islamists in Egypt Back Timing of Military Handover
2012-01-09.abstract: A leader of the Muslim Brotherhood’s political party said it had decided to support keeping the military-appointed cabinet and prime minister in place for six months.

2012-01-09.title: A Revered, and Divided, Liberator of South Africa
2012-01-09.abstract: The African National Congress celebrated its 100th anniversary this weekend at a lavish jamboree, focusing on its revered history rather than bitter schisms that now divide it.

2012-01-10.title: In Brazil, Officers of the Law, Outside the Law
2012-01-10.abstract: In parts of Brazil, militias extort money, punish those who cross them and carry out extrajudicial killings.

2012-01-10.title: Pentagon Tries to Counter Cheap, Potent Weapons
2012-01-10.abstract: President Obama’s new military strategy has focused attention on weapons like mines and cyberattacks.

2012-01-10.title: Peace Talks Are Discussed in a Session in Jordan
2012-01-10.abstract: The encounter between Israeli and Palestinian officials was kept at such a low profile that it was almost as if it had not happened at all.

2012-01-10.title: Iraq: 16 Killed in Multiple Attacks
2012-01-10.abstract: At least 16 people were killed across Iraq on Monday in car bombings, assassinations, roadside bomb blasts and attacks on security forces.

2012-01-10.title: Egypt’s Women Find Power Still Hinges on Men
2012-01-10.abstract: Despite playing a large role in the protests that ousted President Hosni Mubarak, Egyptian women still face the restrictions of a patriarchal system.

2012-01-11.title: Scotland Plans Independence Vote
2012-01-11.abstract: The Scottish government said Tuesday that it would hold a referendum on independence from Britain in 2014.

2012-01-11.title: Britain: Plan Set for Fast Trains
2012-01-11.abstract: The government approved on Tuesday a controversial $51 billion high-speed rail line linking London to Birmingham, Manchester and Leeds.

2012-01-11.title: Italy: Government Official Quits
2012-01-11.abstract: An under secretary to Prime Minister Mario Monti resigned after a report that he had vacationed in 2007 and 2008 as the guest of two businessmen later implicated in a corruption investigation.

2012-01-11.title: Egypt: Mubarak’s Trial Extended
2012-01-11.abstract: Judges in the trial of former President Hosni Mubarak on Tuesday scheduled a full month for his defense team to make its case.

2012-01-11.title: Outsiders Feed Unrest in Syria, Assad Says
2012-01-11.abstract: In a speech, President Bashar al-Assad stressed that he would use an “an iron hand” against foreign enemies, even as many at home and abroad are predicting the fall of his government.

2012-01-12.title: Peru: Suspect in Holloway Case Pleads Guilty in Lima Murder
2012-01-12.abstract: Joran van der Sloot, who became notorious as the prime suspect in the disappearance of Natalee Holloway, pleaded guilty to the 2010 murder of a Peruvian woman he met at a casino and could face 30 years in prison.

2012-01-12.title: Tips on American College Admissions Essays, From a Veteran Dean
2012-01-12.abstract: The dean of admissions of Pomona College, a liberal arts school in California, provides advice for Indian applicants on constructing an effective admissions essay.

2012-01-12.title: Egypt: Anti-Mubarak Uprising to Be Honored With Holiday
2012-01-12.abstract: Egypt’s military rulers declared Jan. 25 an official holiday to celebrate the anniversary of the uprising that ousted President Hosni Mubarak last year and recognize demonstrators killed during the protests.

2012-01-12.title: Latter-Day Dig of ‘Great Escape’ Tunnels Humbles Modern Engineers
2012-01-12.abstract: Engineers, archaeologists and historians tried to uncover the World War II escape tunnels to replicate the ingenuity of the Royal Air Force fliers. They were partly successful.

2012-01-12.title: Mexico Updates Death Toll in Drug War to 47,515, but Critics Dispute the Data
2012-01-12.abstract: The Mexican government has failed to create the tracking system it needs to understand criminal trends and improve security, experts say.

2012-01-13.title: Somalia: Rebels Attack Kenya
2012-01-13.abstract: Somali militants have attacked a Kenyan police post near the Kenya-Somalia border, killing at least six people and kidnapping several others, Kenyan officials said Thursday.

2012-01-13.title: Vote Holds Fate of Nuclear Power in Taiwan
2012-01-13.abstract: The leading challenger in Taiwan’s presidential election wants to end the country’s reliance on nuclear energy, but her critics say that Taiwan lacks feasible alternatives.

2012-01-13.title: Turkish Official Says a Russian Ship, Perhaps With Munitions, Has Reached Syria
2012-01-13.abstract: If the ship is carrying munitions, it would be in defiance of a European Union embargo.

2012-01-13.title: U.S. Sends Top Iranian Leader a Warning on Strait Threat
2012-01-13.abstract: The administration has warned Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, that closing the Strait of Hormuz is a “red line” that would provoke a response, officials said.

2012-01-13.title: The Narrow and Vulnerable Strait of Hormuz
2012-01-13.abstract: The shipping lanes through the Strait of Hormuz are only six miles wide and are vulnerable to attack by Iranian missiles.

2012-01-14.title: A Champion of France’s Downtrodden, With Limits of His Own
2012-01-14.abstract: As a deputy prefect who is mostly paralyzed as a result of a rare genetic degenerative disease, Jean-Christophe Parisot fights for immigrants, the elderly and the poor.

2012-01-14.title: Reprehensible Behavior Is a Risk of Combat, Experts Say
2012-01-14.abstract: Reprehensible behavior, combat veterans and military experts say, is an ever-present risk when troops in their teens and early 20s are thrown into nerve-racking battle for months at a time.

2012-01-14.title: The Urination Video and Pressures of Battle
2012-01-14.abstract: The United States Marine Corps identified the four Marines shown in a video urinating on what appear to be dead Taliban fighters, and has named a lead investigating officer to decide whether they should be charged, Marine officials said. Also, an in-depth look at conduct on the battlefield, a place where reprehensible behavior is an ever-present risk in young soldiers thrown into battle, according to combat veterans and military experts.

2012-01-14.title: Death of Oxford Astrophysicist Evokes Echoes of a TV Drama for the British
2012-01-14.abstract: The location, and the professor’s longtime friendship with a colleague who was at least briefly under suspicion, have added to the comparisons with a police detective series.

2012-01-14.title: Rauf Denktash, Who Led Turkish Cypriots, Dies at 87
2012-01-14.abstract: Mr. Denktash, a former leader of the Turkish minority in Cyprus, for decades successfully opposed reunification of that divided island in pursuit of a separate state for his people.

2012-01-15.title: Fear of Civil War Mounts in Syria as Crisis Deepens
2012-01-15.abstract: The failure of an Arab League mission and a government as defiant as its opposition is in disarray have thrust Syria into what increasingly looks like a protracted and chaotic conflict.

2012-01-15.title: Where the Italian Cruise Ship Ran Aground
2012-01-15.abstract: A preliminary account of the final moments of the luxury cruise ship Costa Concordia, according to the Coast Guard.

2012-01-15.title: Palestinian Authority Leaders Talk With Israel and Hamas
2012-01-15.abstract: The meeting in Gaza between Palestinian factions appeared to make some progress in mending their rift.

2012-01-15.title: Liner Captain Is Questioned in Capsizing Off Italy Coast
2012-01-15.abstract: As the search for survivors continued, the operator of the cruise ship that capsized off the coast of Italy said the captain may be to blame.

2012-01-15.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-01-15.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-01-16.title: As Reforms Flag in Greece, Europe Aims to Limit Damage
2012-01-16.abstract: European officials say the task of negotiations with lenders is less to help Greece through its troubles than to avoid an uncontrolled default that could threaten the global financial system.

2012-01-16.title: Prices and Anger Rise in Nigeria, Presaging More Strikes
2012-01-16.abstract: Nigerians emerged from their homes this weekend to find their fragile economic existence in an even deeper crisis because of a steep increase in the price of gasoline.

2012-01-16.title: In an Informal Economy, Part Valet, Part Hustler
2012-01-16.abstract: In South Africa, where the official unemployment rate is 25 percent, car guarding has proved a powerful draw for young men with little prospect of a formal job.

2012-01-16.title: Flexing Muscle, Baghdad Detains U.S. Contractors
2012-01-16.abstract: The crackdown comes amid other moves by the government to take over functions once performed by the United States military and to claim areas of the country it had controlled.

2012-01-16.title: A Changed Way of War in Afghanistan’s Skies
2012-01-16.abstract: The use of air power has changed markedly during the long Afghan conflict, reflecting the political costs and sensitivities of civilian casualties and the increasing use of aerial drones.

2012-01-17.title: A Gift Fit for a Queen, and a Round of Ridicule
2012-01-17.abstract: A leaked copy of a letter written by Britain’s education minister suggested that a new yacht would be a fitting way for the nation to mark the queen’s 60th anniversary on the throne.

2012-01-17.title: Frenchwomen Worry About Suspect Breast Implants
2012-01-17.abstract: About 30,000 women were given the implants, which are more susceptible to leaks and rupture, and many fear for their health.

2012-01-17.title: Where the Cruise Ship Crashed
2012-01-17.abstract: The Costa Concordia cruise ship that partially sank near an Italian island may have been caused by human error.

2012-01-17.title: Belgian Church Offices Raided in Abuse Inquiry
2012-01-17.abstract: Belgian officials removed files in an effort to establish whether there were grounds to prosecute priests on charges of negligence and failing to aid abuse victims.

2012-01-17.title: Struggling, Egypt Seeks Loan From I.M.F.
2012-01-17.abstract: With the Egyptian economy suffering from the collapse of tourism and foreign investment amid the unrest and uncertainty, officials make seek even more.

2012-01-18.title: Iran Tightens Its Security for Scientists After Killing
2012-01-18.abstract: An assassination last week was the fifth since 2007 involving a scientist with connections to the country’s disputed nuclear program.

2012-01-18.title: Egypt: Mubarak Defense Is Opened
2012-01-18.abstract: Lawyers for Hosni Mubarak, the ousted president, began laying out their defense against charges that he participated in corruption and directed the killing of demonstrators.

2012-01-18.title: At South Pole, a Game of Cricket
2012-01-18.abstract: British adventurers played cricket on Tuesday at the South Pole to commemorate the centenary of the arrival there of the ill-fated expedition led by Robert Falcon Scott.

2012-01-18.title: U.S. Says Sudan Is Blocking Aid
2012-01-18.abstract: The United States and Sudan traded accusations over the humanitarian situation in the states of Blue Nile and South Kordofan.

2012-01-18.title: Canada Accuses Naval Officer of Sharing State Secrets
2012-01-18.abstract: Court documents assert that Sub-Lt. Jeffery Paul Delisle, who worked in key military intelligence centers, began spying four and a half years ago.

2012-01-19.title: Cruise Lines Use Law and Contracts to Limit Liability
2012-01-19.abstract: Cruise lines are protected by international law and by a carefully worded contract that passengers accept when they buy tickets.

2012-01-19.title: Financial Aid Advice for Indian Students From Colgate University
2012-01-19.abstract: Colgate awarded nearly $6 million in institutional aid to international students last year.

2012-01-19.title: The Literary Circus Comes to Jaipur
2012-01-19.abstract: Page Two columnist Manu Joseph, writing about  the five-day Jaipur Literature Festival that opens on Friday, argues that British and American publishers, have caused “a surge of Indian writers who are trying to sell the great Indian exotica to white people, and they guess that what the foreigners love is tradition, poverty, wedding scenes, burning widows, rebirths and talking monkeys.”

2012-01-19.title: China: Dissident Author Flees to U.S.
2012-01-19.abstract: Yu Jie, a prominent writer and a critic of the Chinese Communist Party, said Wednesday in a news conference in Washington that he and his family had left China on Jan. 11 after more than a year of harassment and house arrest.

2012-01-19.title: Britain: Rendition Inquiry Put on Hold
2012-01-19.abstract: Justice Minister Kenneth Clarke said that the inquiry over whether the secret intelligence service, MI6, was complicit in the abuse of terrorism suspects after Sept. 11 could not proceed until all related criminal investigations were completed.

2012-01-20.title: Russia: Election Official Offers to Resign
2012-01-20.abstract: The country’s top election official, Vladimir Y. Churov, accused of overseeing a fraudulent parliamentary election last month, submitted his resignation on Thursday, Russian news agencies reported.

2012-01-20.title: Rwanda: 4 Military Officers Arrested
2012-01-20.abstract: The senior officers were placed under house arrest on counts of indiscipline and illegal business dealings in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, the military said Thursday.

2012-01-20.title: Spain’s Central Government to Rush Aid to Financially Ailing Regions
2012-01-20.abstract: A $10 billion effort to shore up the regional governments is part of a move to tighten controls over their spending.

2012-01-20.title: Putin Welcomes Kissinger: ‘Old Friends’ to Talk Shop
2012-01-20.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin of Russia will meet with former Secretary of State Henry A. Kissinger to discuss world affairs, a reunion of two “old friends,” a Putin aide said.

2012-01-20.title: Italy Finds a Heroic Foil for Its Scorned Captain
2012-01-20.abstract: Observers say a national propensity for dualism has led to a Coast Guard officer’s portrayal as a hero against the captain of the Costa Concordia.

2012-01-21.title: Chinese Poets, Public Enemies
2012-01-21.abstract: Poetry, it is now clear, can be a felony in China — just as it was in authoritarian regimes of yore. Officials in Hangzhou, in eastern China, have charged Zhu Yufu with subversion of the state for a poem he wrote last year called “It’s Time.”

2012-01-21.title: A Brazilian Magnate Points to Himself for Inspiration
2012-01-21.abstract: Eike Batista, Brazil’s richest man, wants to teach his country to look up to its entrepreneurs the way Americans do.

2012-01-21.title: Prison Death Brings Outcry Against Cuba
2012-01-21.abstract: Human rights advocates and American officials criticized the government for limiting political freedom after a dissident who had carried out a hunger strike died.

2012-01-21.title: The Caucus: Republican Candidates at the Citadel
2012-01-21.abstract: In the prelude to South Carolina’s Republican primary on Saturday, the candidates have trying to engage students of the state’s military college, the Citadel.

2012-01-21.title: China Leader Warns Iran Not to Make Nuclear Arms
2012-01-21.abstract: Prime Minister Wen Jiabao, visiting the Middle East, said China “adamantly opposes Iran developing and possessing nuclear weapons” — unusually pointed language for Chinese diplomacy.

2012-01-22.title: Chávez Gets Bluster Back and Reclaims the Spotlight
2012-01-22.abstract: After a year of cancer treatment and facing a tough re-election campaign, President Hugo Chávez is trying to show he is still the dominant figure in Venezuelan politics.

2012-01-22.title: How to Keep the Dear Leader Well Preserved
2012-01-22.abstract: Kim Jong-il has been dead for a month, and the embalmers are said to be at their work. Reports from Moscow say that Russian scientists are guiding North Korean doctors in giving Mr. Kim the same treatments that have kept Vladimir Lenin in the pink since 1924.

2012-01-22.title: How Do You Say ‘Mormon’ in French?
2012-01-22.abstract: What hurt Mitt Romney more in South Carolina, his Mormonism or his French? It just so happens that France, where Romney served as a missionary, is about to get its first Mormon temple. But there is resistance. Religion and politics. Is it  la maladie américaine? Or a test of tolerance?

2012-01-22.title: In India, Oprah Woos Crowd
2012-01-22.abstract: The media mogul was a big draw at the Jaipur Literature Festival.

2012-01-22.title: At Jaipur Fest, Difficult Balancing Act
2012-01-22.abstract: Free speech and safety both considerations.

2012-01-23.title: Pakistan Court Widens Role, Stirring Fears for Stability
2012-01-23.abstract: Pakistan’s Supreme Court has inserted itself as the third player in a bruising confrontation between military and civilian leaders at a time when Pakistan faces a dizzying array of threats.

2012-01-23.title: The Emerging Gilded Age
2012-01-23.abstract: It is a season of doubt and fear in much of the West, as the relative decline of Europe and the United States seems more and more apparent. But, Chrystia Freeland writes, the current era of global prosperity owes its life to Western policies and technology. How much of China and India’s success is rooted in the West?

2012-01-23.title: Rising Strife Threatens Tenuous Iraqi Stability
2012-01-23.abstract: With political and sectarian factions battling for power and influence following the American withdrawal, violence and instability have escalated across the country.

2012-01-23.title: Arab League Floats Ambitious New Peace Plan for Syria
2012-01-23.abstract: Faced with the failure of its observer mission to curb the violence in Syria, the Arab League offered a proposal that would require President Bashar al-Assad to hand over power.

2012-01-23.title: In Egypt, Signs of Accord Between Military Council and Islamists
2012-01-23.abstract: Evident points of agreement on a new charter include a legal system no more Islamic than the previous one and broad guarantees of freedom of religion and expression.

2012-01-24.title: Year of the Dragon
2012-01-24.abstract: Across China and around the world, people are celebrating the Lunar New Year and the advent of the Year of the Dragon. Five filmmakers captured the spirit and sparkle of the celebrations in Beijing.

2012-01-24.title: Greece Publishes List of 4,000 Tax Scofflaws
2012-01-24.abstract: The Greek government published a list of more than 4,000 people, including several household names, who owe a total of $19 billion to the cash-strapped state.

2012-01-24.title: U.N. Investigates Allegations Against Troops in Haiti
2012-01-24.abstract: The United Nations is investigating allegations against peacekeepers in Haiti involving two cases of sexual abuse of minors, the organization’s spokesman said Monday.

2012-01-24.title: France: Government Bans Domestic Islamist Group
2012-01-24.abstract: The French government said that the banned group was a terrorist organization training its members “for armed combat,” Interior Minister Claude Guéant said Monday.

2012-01-24.title: India: 2 Arrested in July Terrorist Attack on Mumbai
2012-01-24.abstract: Authorities in Mumbai announced the arrest of two suspects on Monday in connection with a terrorist attack last July that killed 27 people in bombings across the city.

2012-01-25.title: TimesCast | Egypt's Revolution Continues
2012-01-25.abstract: January 24, 2012 — Robert Mackey of the Lede blog interviews Mosa’ab Elshamy, an activist and photographer living in Cairo, about the first anniversary of Egypt’s revolution.

2012-01-25.title: Anger in Iraq After Plea Bargain Over 2005 Massacre
2012-01-25.abstract: Iraqis were outraged to learn that the Marine considered the ringleader of the 2005 killings in Haditha was sentenced on Tuesday to a reduction in rank but avoided any jail time.

2012-01-25.title: Learning Amid the Ruins
2012-01-25.abstract: Poor Cambodian villagers come to Siem Reap to work in the tourism industry around the ruins of Angkor Wat, but many also find a path to higher education.

2012-01-25.title: In Brazil, Fears of a Slide Back for Amazon Protection
2012-01-25.abstract: Since Dilma Rousseff was elected president, the government has shifted its stance on the Amazon to side more with agricultural interests.

2012-01-25.title: Director Quits After Changes at Global Fund
2012-01-25.abstract: The executive director of the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria resigned after two months of struggle over the future direction of the fund.

2012-01-26.title: An Indian Student’s Guide to the SAT and ACT Admissions Exams
2012-01-26.abstract: Robin Mamlet, a former dean of admissions at Stanford University in California, and Christine VanDeVelde, a journalist, explain the alphabet of tests standing between applicants and their entry to American colleges.

2012-01-26.title: Pattern of Intimidation Is Seen in Arrests of Iranian Journalists and Bloggers
2012-01-26.abstract: Rights groups and people who know the detained journalists said the government apparently wanted word of the arrests to spread informally, to heighten the atmosphere of fear and paranoia.

2012-01-26.title: Djibouti Outpost Behind Somalia Rescue Is Part of New Defense Strategy
2012-01-26.abstract: An austere base, Camp Lemonnier, is part of an archipelago of outposts in high-risk environments that can preserve a military presence at relatively low cost.

2012-01-26.title: Potent Sting Is Prepared in the Belly of a Warship
2012-01-26.abstract: Deep below the water line, sailors on the aircraft carrier John C. Stennis build the bombs that turn the ship from a floating airport into a projection of American military power.

2012-01-26.title: Russia: Election Monitor Group Faces Threat of Eviction
2012-01-26.abstract: Russia’s only independent elections monitor, Golos, has been told to either find new office space or expect continual power failures from now until March 6, severely hampering the group’s ability to monitor the presidential election on March 4.

2012-01-27.title: Long a Divisive Figure, but Still the Grocer’s Daughter Back Home
2012-01-27.abstract: Long after her departure from public life, Margaret Thatcher, the former British prime minister who is the subject of a new film, still divides the British, even in her hometown.

2012-01-27.title: Papua New Guinea: Mutiny Said to Fail
2012-01-27.abstract: The announcement comes amid escalating tensions between the current and former heads of government in the South Pacific island nation, each of whom claims legitimacy.

2012-01-27.title: Israel Senses Bluffing in Iran’s Threats of Retaliation
2012-01-27.abstract: As Israel’s political and military leaders weigh options to stop Iran’s nuclear program, many are guided by an assessment that an attack is unlikely to set off widespread conflict.

2012-01-27.title: For Many in Pakistan, a Television Show Goes Too Far
2012-01-27.abstract: Nongovernmental organizations are suing a station over a show featuring a group of women on the hunt for “immoral” behavior.

2012-01-27.title: In Britain, a Partial List of Those Who Declined to Be Called ‘Sir’
2012-01-27.abstract: The government on Friday released the names of 277 people who rejected the rarefied opportunity to become knights, dames and the like between 1951 and 1999.

2012-01-28.title: Teaching Tibetan Ways, a School in China Is an Unlikely Wonder
2012-01-28.abstract: Far from the eyes of Beijing technocrats, students learn to write Tibetan and get their first formal introduction to a history, culture and religion that many call embattled.

2012-01-28.title: Iran Says It Could Terminate European Oil Sales Next Week
2012-01-28.abstract: Escalating retaliatory threats over nuclear sanctions, Iran also advised Arab oil producers that any attempt by them to replace the exports would be considered unfriendly.

2012-01-28.title: Senegal Court Says President Can Run Again
2012-01-28.abstract: The country’s constitutional court ruled on Friday that President Abdoulaye Wade could seek a third term, and threw out the candidacy of Youssou N’Dour, a popular singer.

2012-01-28.title: As Nations Line Up Against Syrian Government, Russia Sides Firmly With Assad
2012-01-28.abstract: Russia remains a staunch defender of Syria, providing it with a political lifeline as well as arms and ammunition.

2012-01-28.title: Russia: Liberal Candidate Is Barred From Election
2012-01-28.abstract: The Central Election Commission on Friday barred Grigory A. Yavlinsky, founder of the liberal opposition party Yabloko, from competing in the March 4 presidential election.

2012-01-29.title: In Rankous, Barely Holding On
2012-01-29.abstract: Free Syria Army tries to hold on to Rankous, a town located near Damascus.

2012-01-29.title: Yemeni Leader Arrives in U.S. for Medical Treatment
2012-01-29.abstract: President Ali Abdullah Saleh of Yemen, who is seeking treatment for injuries sustained when the presidential palace was bombed in June, arrived on Saturday, officials said.

2012-01-29.title: Homai Vyarawalla, Pioneering Indian Photojournalist, Dies at 98
2012-01-29.abstract: Ms. Vyarawalla was hailed as the first Indian woman to work as a photojournalist and for chronicling the country’s march toward independence.

2012-01-29.title: It’s No Beijing, But Hong Kong Is Choking, Too
2012-01-29.abstract: Air pollution levels in Beijing and other Chinese cities have started to worry — and anger — many of the country’s 1.3 billion citizens. By Beijing’s standards, Hong Kong’s air is probably sparkling clean, which is damning with faint praise.

2012-01-29.title: When Tibetan Despair Leads to Self-Immolation
2012-01-29.abstract: Why do they do it, these Buddhist monks and nuns and other young Tibetans? Why would they drink a cup of kerosene, then douse themselves and strike a match? What is behind these horrifying self-immolations, especially since Buddhism forbids suicide and the Dalai Lama himself has preached against it?

2012-01-30.title: Jonathan Idema, Con Man and Afghan Bounty Hunter, Dies at 55
2012-01-30.abstract: Mr. Idema gained notoriety in post-invasion Afghanistan as a swaggering hunter of terrorists, then ignominy when he was imprisoned for taking Afghans hostage and torturing them.

2012-01-30.title: To Mend Ties After Clash, Kazakhstan Makes an Offer
2012-01-30.abstract: In the six weeks since police officers killed 17 oil workers on strike, senior officials have acknowledged errors and begun a sweeping program to offer jobs to the strikers.

2012-01-30.title: U.S. Drones Patrolling Its Skies Provoke Outrage in Iraq
2012-01-30.abstract: The use of unarmed craft to protect American buildings and personnel may foreshadow an expansion of such operations to the United States government’s diplomatic arm.

2012-01-30.title: Afghan Family, Led by Father Who Called Girls a Disgrace, Is Guilty of Murder
2012-01-30.abstract: Mohammad Shafia, who disapproved of his daughters’ wearing revealing clothes and dating, was convicted in Canada with his wife and a son of killing the girls and another wife.

2012-01-30.title: Iraq’s Sunni Leaders End Their Boycott
2012-01-30.abstract: Iraq’s Sunni leaders on Sunday said that they would end their boycott of Parliament, easing tensions in the country’s political crisis.

2012-01-31.title: Burmese Civil War Turns Grimmer for a Guerrilla
2012-01-31.abstract: Maj. Robin Maran, a commander for the ethnic Kachin minority, is facing a stronger and fiercer Burmese Army as a civil war has resumed.

2012-01-31.title: Tunisia Faces a Balancing Act of Democracy and Religion
2012-01-31.abstract: A blasphemy trial in Tunis symbolizes an emotional struggle, with implications for the Arab world, playing out with the rise of Islamists after the end of a secular dictatorship.

2012-01-31.title: Food Crisis as Drought and Cold Hit Mexico
2012-01-31.abstract: Government officials said the drought is the worst since record-keeping began and has left two million people without access to water and devastated cropland in nearly half of the country.

2012-01-31.title: Afghan Officials Consider Own Talks With Taliban
2012-01-31.abstract: President Hamid Karzai is said to fear being sidelined by a United States initiative for peace negotiations with the Taliban and is pushing to open direct talks, Afghan officials said.

2012-01-31.title: In a Political Ritual, Candidates Tour France in a Race for 500 Signatures
2012-01-31.abstract: France’s political system requires 500 mayors and local officials to sign their names in support of a presidential candidate before he or she can be put on the ballot.
